evals.name: feature-payment-retry
evals.prompt: Hey, I need a spec for adding payment retry logic to our billing service. Right now when a charge fails we just cancel the subscription immediately which is pretty aggressive — we're losing like 8% of subscribers to transient Stripe failures. We want to retry failed charges a few times before giving up. The codebase is at /Users/josh/.claude/skills/spec-workspace/test-project/. No .specs/ directory exists yet.
evals.expected_output: A feature spec following the template with: probing questions about retry strategy, marker annotations for unknowns, real file paths from the codebase, .specs/ directory initialized, vague language caught if any
expectations: The spec file is created at .specs/features/ with YYYY-MM-DD naming convention
The .specs/ directory is initialized (init-specs.sh called or equivalent)
The output contains at least one [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] or [ASSUMPTION] marker
The Technical Approach section references real files from the codebase (e.g., charge.go, subscription.go)
The spec includes an Out of Scope / Non-Goals section with at least one item
The spec includes an Alternatives Considered section with at least one alternative
The Verification section contains the IMPLEMENTING AGENT instruction comment
Discovery probing questions are asked before generating the spec draft
The spec identifies that ChargeResult is consumed by analytics, fraud-detection, and admin dashboard

evals.name: bugfix-upgrade-double-charge
evals.prompt: We have a bug report — when a user tries to upgrade their plan and the prorated charge fails, they end up on the new plan without paying. Basically getting a free upgrade. Can you write a bugfix spec? The codebase is at /Users/josh/.claude/skills/spec-workspace/test-project/.
evals.expected_output: A bugfix spec using the bugfix template that identifies the root cause in subscription.go (plan updated before charge succeeds) with specific file:line references
expectations: The spec file is created at .specs/bugs/ with YYYY-MM-DD naming convention
The spec uses the bugfix template structure (Bug Description, Root Cause, Fix Approach, etc.)
The Root Cause section identifies subscription.go and the plan-update-before-charge ordering issue
The Fix Approach recommends moving the plan update to after the charge succeeds
The spec includes a Severity field
The Verification section includes at least one automated test assertion
The spec explicitly scopes out unrelated refactoring in Out of Scope

evals.name: domain-doc-billing
evals.prompt: Can you create an AGENTS.md domain doc for the billing module? I want AI agents to understand the gotchas and cross-boundary stuff without having to read every file. The codebase is at /Users/josh/.claude/skills/spec-workspace/test-project/.
evals.expected_output: A per-directory AGENTS.md file in internal/billing/ that follows the cardinal rule (no code-in-English), stays under 50 lines, and documents gotchas and cross-boundary behavior
expectations: The AGENTS.md file is created at internal/billing/AGENTS.md (next to the code, not in .specs/)
The document is 50 lines or fewer
The document does NOT restate function signatures or type definitions from the source code
The document mentions the immediate-cancellation-on-failure behavior as a gotcha
The document mentions that ChargeResult is consumed by external systems (analytics, fraud-detection, admin dashboard)
The document mentions the UpgradePlan bug or the plan-update ordering issue
The user is asked whether they use Cursor (for .cursor/rules/*.mdc integration)

evals.name: mini-spec-timeout-config
evals.prompt: Quick one — the Stripe client timeout is hardcoded to 30 seconds in main.go. I want to make it configurable via an environment variable. Single file change basically. Codebase: /Users/josh/.claude/skills/spec-workspace/test-project/.
evals.expected_output: A mini-spec (not a full feature spec) that includes only Problem, Scope, Technical Approach, and Verification sections. The model should recognize this is a small change and offer a mini-spec.
expectations: The model offers or uses a mini-spec format (not the full feature template)
The spec references stripe_client.go and/or main.go as the files to modify
The spec is noticeably shorter than a full feature spec
The spec includes a Verification section with at least one testable condition
The spec does NOT include extensive probing questions (proportional to the small change)

evals.name: vague-input-edge-case
evals.prompt: We need to make the billing system more robust and scalable. It should also be fast and handle errors gracefully. Can you spec this out? Codebase: /Users/josh/.claude/skills/spec-workspace/test-project/.
evals.expected_output: The model should catch the vague language ('robust', 'scalable', 'fast', 'gracefully') and push back with clarifying questions rather than blindly generating a spec with vague requirements
expectations: The model identifies at least 2 vague terms (robust, scalable, fast, gracefully) and asks for concrete definitions
The model does NOT generate a full spec with vague terms left unquantified
If a draft is generated, vague terms are replaced with [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers demanding specific metrics
The model asks clarifying questions before or during spec generation
The output demonstrates the vague language lint behavior described in the skill
